user_stories.title: Review AI-generated changes before merge
user_stories.story: As a team developer, I want the workflow to pause after AI code review so that I can review all changes before they are merged to main.

acceptance_criteria.description: When code_review.enabled == true and GATE-08 passes, workflow enters paused_for_review state
When code_review.enabled == false, workflow proceeds to merge without pause
The active_workflow status shows awaiting_human_review
Workflow does not advance or merge until user explicitly approves or bypasses

user_stories.title: Pull Request creation for git projects
user_stories.story: As a developer using git, I want a Pull Request to be created automatically when the review pause activates so that I can review changes using my standard PR workflow.

acceptance_criteria.description: If git repo and gh CLI available, create PR via gh pr create
PR title format: [REQ-NNNN] Feature description or [BUG-NNNN] Fix description
PR body includes the review summary content
If gh not available, fall back to document-only review with manual PR instruction
PR URL recorded in active_workflow.review.pr_url in state.json
If gh available but PR creation fails (network, permissions), fall back to document-only review, log error, instruct user to create PR manually

user_stories.title: Review request for non-git projects
user_stories.story: As a developer not using git, I want a review request document generated so that I can still review AI changes before they are applied.

acceptance_criteria.description: review-request.md created in docs/requirements/{artifact_folder}/
Document contains all PR-equivalent information
Document content presented to user in terminal

user_stories.title: See all artifacts and changed files in review
user_stories.story: As a reviewer, I want the review request to include links to all generated documentation and all changed files so that I have full context for my review.

acceptance_criteria.description: All artifacts from phase status in state.json listed with relative paths
git diff main...HEAD --name-only used to list changed files for git projects
For non-git projects, artifact list from state.json used
Files grouped by category: Documentation, Source Code, Tests, Configuration

user_stories.title: Review summary document
user_stories.story: As a reviewer, I want a comprehensive review summary document saved so that I have a persistent record of what was reviewed and the outcome.

acceptance_criteria.description: File saved at docs/requirements/{artifact_folder}/review-summary.md
Contains: feature description, workflow type, phases, gates, artifacts, files, tests, constitutional compliance
Document displayed to user when review pause activates
Bypass reason appended if review is bypassed

user_stories.title: Bypass review with mandatory comment
user_stories.story: As a developer, I want the option to bypass the code review with a mandatory explanation so that I can proceed in urgent situations while maintaining an audit trail.

acceptance_criteria.description: User presented with [A] Approve, [B] Bypass, [R] Reject options
Bypass requires non-empty comment (minimum 10 characters)
Empty or too-short comments rejected with re-entry prompt
Bypass reason recorded in active_workflow.review.bypass_reason
Bypass reason appended to review summary document
Bypass event logged to state.json history array
If [R] Reject selected, workflow cancelled with reason 'rejected at human review'. Branch preserved (not deleted). Equivalent to /sdlc cancel.

user_stories.title: Automatic activation for teams
user_stories.story: As a team lead, I want the code review feature to automatically activate when the team has more than one developer so that no AI changes bypass human review.

acceptance_criteria.description: state.json contains code_review section: { enabled: false, team_size: 1 }
When team_size > 1, enabled automatically set to true by installer
When team_size == 1, enabled defaults to false
enabled field can be manually overridden regardless of team size

user_stories.title: Team size captured during installation
user_stories.story: As a developer installing iSDLC, I want to be asked about team size so that the code review feature is configured correctly from the start.

acceptance_criteria.description: Installer asks: How many developers will work on this project? (1)
Default value is 1 (solo developer)
Response stored in state.json under code_review.team_size
If team_size > 1, code_review.enabled set to true and user informed
Non-numeric input defaults to 1

user_stories.title: Configuration changeable after installation
user_stories.story: As a developer whose team has grown, I want to change the team size and enable code review without reinstalling so that the feature adapts to team changes.

acceptance_criteria.description: code_review section in state.json is the single source of truth
User can modify code_review.enabled and team_size at any time
Changes take effect on next workflow run (no restart)
isdlc update preserves existing code_review configuration

user_stories.title: Reminder when code review is bypassed
user_stories.story: As a developer on a growing team, I want to see a reminder on every commit that code review is bypassed so that I am prompted to enable it when the team grows.

acceptance_criteria.description: Hook fires on commit-related events
If enabled == false AND team_size > 1, display warning message
If enabled == false AND team_size == 1, no message displayed
Message suggests enabling code review if team has grown
Hook does not block the commit (fail-open)
Hook completes in < 100ms
